items.acceptanceCriteria: Equipment documentation is available
Previous inspection reports are checked
Daily pre-operational inspection checklists are checked (if applicable)
Preventive Maintenance is established and updated records are maintained
Equipment number is clearly marked for identification purposes
Crane is painted safety yellow
Crane is painted safety yellow-and-black stripes on offshore facilities
A sign is posted warning the operator not to rely solely on any automatic device as a substitute for safe operating practice
Safe working load is clearly marked on the runway and the lifting machine (Arabic & English)
Controls are clearly marked with their functions and modes of operation
The load block is inched into its limit device or run in at slow speed on multispeed or variable-speed hoists
Hoist drum specifications are marked (rated load, drum size, rope size, rope speed (ft/min or m/s), rated power)
Hand Chain Hoist: Manufacturer data, serial number and safe working load are clearly displayed on the item
Electric Powered Hoist: Manufacturer data, serial number, safe working load, voltage and phase are clearly displayed on the item
Air Powered Hoist: Manufacturer data, serial number, model, safe working load and rated air pressure are clearly displayed on the item
Warning signs/labels are provided on the hoist units and electrical enclosures
Tanks, valves, pumps, lines, and other parts of air or hydraulic systems have no leakage
Structure is vibration free under normal operating condition
Monorail end stops are installed and in good condition
Jib crane end stops are installed and in good condition
Tracks are properly installed and aligned
Crane runways or monorail tracks are fastened and Secured to a supporting structure
All welded members are free of defects and not corroded
Guards protect moving parts such as gears, chains, chain sprockets
Guards protect ropes where liable to come in contact with conductors
Guards are provided to prevent contact between crane bridge or runway conductors and hoisting ropes.
Hand chain operated hoist : Hoist automatically stops and holds lifted load when the actuating force is removed
Electric Powered Hoist : Braking system will stop and hold the load hook when controls are released under any load condition
Air Powered Hoist : Braking system will stop and hold the load hook when controls are released under any load condition
An electric hoist stops and holds the load block in the event of power failure
An air hoist stops and holds the load block in the event of air pressure loose
For Electric Powered Hoist: is Braking systems has means for adjustment to compensate for wear
For, Air Powered Hoist: is Braking systems has means for adjustment to compensate for wear
Control circuit voltage does not exceed 600v for AC or DC
Pendant control station is constructed to prevent electrical conductors strain
Push button enclosure is grounded
Push button enclosure is marked for identification of function
Parts of electrical equipment are enclosed and are not exposed to inadvertent contact under normal operating conditions
Live parts of electrical equipment are protected from direct exposure to grease and oil and protected from dirt and moisture
Guards on live parts are not deformed or/and in contact
Floor operated cranes controllers return to off position when released
Pendant push buttons that control motion return to off position when pressure is released

sections.title: Load chain, Rope and Hook Block

items.acceptanceCriteria: Chain passes over all load sprockets without binding
Hand Operated Chain : Chain length for extension (stretch) tolerance is not longer than 2.5% of unused chain or as per manufacturer recommendations
Power Operated Chain : Chain length for extension (stretch) tolerance is not longer than 1.5% of unused chain or as per manufacturer recommendations
The chain does not suffer from gouges, nicks, corrosion, weld spatter or distorted links (Judgement to be used as to the suitability or otherwise of using chain with these deficiencies)
Chain shall be pitched (calibrated) so as to pass over all load sprockets without binding.
The chain is not stretched or elongated more than 1/4" (6.3 mm) in 12" (305 mm) with reference to the manufacturer's manual (roller chain)
The chain is not twisted more than 15 degree in 5 ft (1.52 m) sections (roller chain)
The roller chain pins, links and rollers move freely and are not corroded, pitted, discolored or damaged
Fitted sling or chain would be retained slack in the bowl of the hook where latches are provided
Hand operated hoist : Load block is provided with a guard against load chain jamming in the load block under normal operating conditions
Electric or Air Powered Hoist : Load block is of the enclosed type and means is provided to guard against rope or load chain jamming in the load block under normal operating conditions.

Removal criteria for running rope over length of 6 × nominal rope diameter: ≤6 strands, 19 class within 3 single strand across 6 strands >6 strands, 19 class within 4 single strand across 8 strands ≤6 strands, 36 class within 5 single strand across 10 strands >6 strands, 36 class within 6 single strand across 12 strands
Removal criteria for rotation-resistant over length of 6 × nominal rope diameter: Category 1, across 6 strands. Category 2, across 2 strands. Category 3, across 2 strands.
Removal criteria for the rope replacement, reductions from nominal diameter greater than 5% at any location
Rope termination is completed at the hoist wedge anchor with a drop forged U- clip
Thimbles shall have a minimum diameter at the bearing surface of at least two times the rope diameter.
Electric and air powered hoists : Rope drum is grooved and free of surface defects that could cause rope damage (excluding hoists made for special applications)
Hoist drum is adequately lubricated as per the hoist manufacturers manual
Drum capacity can accommodate the specific rope size and length
Drum has a minimum of two wraps of rope on it
Each drum end of the rope is anchored by a clamp attached to the drum or by a socket arrangement (approved by the manufacturer)
Drum flanges always extend a minimum of 1/2" (13mm) above the top layer of rope at all times
Labeling and manufacturer data are available and legible
Safe working load is clearly marked on the hook
Hook is not bent or twisted
Hook is not distorted in the throat opening • Max. allowable throat opening is 5% not to exceed 1∕4 in. (6 mm) or as recommended by the manufacturer
Maximum wear in the hook bowl is not exceeding 10% (compared to new hook) or as per manufacturer recommendations
Hook is not cracked, gouged or shows nicks
Hook can lock (if it is a self-locking hook)
inoperative latch (if provided) — any damaged latch or malfunctioning latch that does not close the hook’s throat
Hook latch shall be closed and shall not be used to support any part of the load
Hook is free to rotate
